title: Polish — camera shake / spawn pop + agent free-cam + audio dial + snapshot history

stories.title: Kaboom Crew adopts S091 named easings — death tip + camera shake decay
stories.summary: death-animation-system.ts now imports `easingCurves` from engine/core/systems/tween-system.ts; `deathFallPitch()` samples `easingCurves.easeOutBack(t)` instead of the previous inline `1 - (1 - t) * (1 - t)` — dying bombers tip past 90° (~110% of target) and rock back to settle. camera-shake-system.ts dropped the `decayPerSecond` knob in favor of `durationSeconds` (default 0.45 s); a new pure helper `cameraShakeEnvelope(elapsed, duration)` returns `1 - easingCurves.easeOutElastic(t)`, which opens at 1 and dips below zero mid-curve before settling at 0 — gives the shake a small bouncy stop instead of monotonic exponential fade. The system tracks `peakIntensity` + `elapsed` per shake window; a fresh BlastEvent resets `elapsed` so the bounce stays in sync with the latest detonation.
verification: acceptance: A dying bomber visibly tips past 90° and settles back (theatrical overshoot, not a smooth monotonic glide). Camera shake on a nearby blast stops with a tiny oscillation rather than fading evenly.
examples/kaboom-crew/src/systems/death-animation-system.ts: easingCurves.easeOutBack drives deathFallPitch
examples/kaboom-crew/src/systems/camera-shake-system.ts: cameraShakeEnvelope exported; shake intensity sampled from 1 - easingCurves.easeOutElastic over the active shake window
+2 unit tests on deathFallPitch: peak sample exceeds Math.PI/2 by <15%, pre-peak segment is monotonic, endpoint lands at Math.PI/2
+3 unit tests on cameraShakeEnvelope: endpoints 0/1 exact, defensive duration<=0 returns 0, mid-curve dips below 0 (bounce signature)
Existing camera-shake tests updated to track durationSeconds option (decayPerSecond removed)

stories.title: Bomb / pickup mesh scale 0 → 1 with easeOutBack on spawn
stories.summary: bomb-placement-system.ts + pickup-spawn-system.ts now stamp `Transform.scale = [0, 0, 0]` on the freshly-spawned entity and attach a `Tweens` component driving scale to the final size over 200 ms with `easeOutBack`. The engine's TweenSystem (registered by start.ts) advances each tween in fixedUpdate and removes the Tween component on completion — no per-project advance code needed. Final scales: bombs `[0.35, 0.35, 0.35]`, pickups inherit the per-kind PICKUP_VISUAL.scale.
verification: acceptance: New bombs visibly pop into existence (scale jumps from 0 to slightly past 1 then settles to full); pickups dropped on a softblock-destroyed cell do the same — no longer flash in at full size.
bomb-placement-system.ts: stamps Tweens[{component:'Transform', property:'scale', from:[0,0,0], to:[0.35,0.35,0.35], duration:0.2, ease:'easeOutBack'}]
pickup-spawn-system.ts: same shape, `to` matches per-kind visual.scale
+2 unit tests verifying the Tween component shape on both spawn paths

stories.title: Agent-driven free-fly camera for debugging — POST /__agf/render/freecam
stories.summary: Add an opt-in free-fly camera the agent can position arbitrarily without touching the game's camera control. Surface: `runtime.renderer.setFreeCam({ position, lookAt } | null)` — when non-null, the renderer creates (and pins active) a debug PerspectiveCamera at the given pose; when null, the project's normal active camera takes over again. Dev-bridge: `POST /__agf/render/freecam { position, lookAt }` + `POST /__agf/render/freecam { off: true }` + `GET /__agf/render/freecam`. Useful for inspecting blast aftermath, off-screen entities, prefab layout. Pure agent observability, not a player feature.
verification: acceptance: POST /__agf/render/freecam { position: [10, 8, 10], lookAt: [0, 0, 0] } moves the rendered view to that pose immediately; POST { off: true } restores the previous active camera and the player can keep playing.
engine/render/three-render-adapter.ts: setFreeCam() owns a private PerspectiveCamera; `pickAtNdc` and CameraSyncSystem must observe the override
Dev-bridge route gets a row in docs/agent-probes.md
+ unit test on a fake adapter scene verifying that setFreeCam swaps the active camera and clearing it restores the prior one

stories.title: AudioBus grows a master volume dial — POST /__agf/audio/master-volume
stories.summary: engine/runtime/audio/audio-bus.ts grew `setMasterVolume(value): number` + `getMasterVolume(): number`. Master multiplies per-call volume in `play()`: `el.volume = clamp(master * (volume ?? 1), 0, 1)`. Non-finite inputs to setMasterVolume are ignored. Plumbed through src/app.ts (getAudioMasterVolume / setAudioMasterVolume), window.__agf, and GET/POST /__agf/audio/master-volume on the dev bridge (AGF_BRIDGE_INVALID_AUDIO_VOLUME on non-number).
verification: acceptance: POST /__agf/audio/master-volume { value: 0 } silences subsequent plays; POST { value: 1 } restores; per-play `volume: 0.5` plays at 50% of master.
engine/runtime/audio/audio-bus.ts: setMasterVolume / getMasterVolume on the AudioBus interface; play() applies master * per-call
+3 unit tests in tests/unit/audio-bus.test.ts: getMasterVolume default 1 + clamp + non-finite ignored; master * per-call multiplication; final clamp at 1.0 against excessive per-call volume

stories.title: Briefly duck SFX while a match-* chime plays
stories.summary: examples/kaboom-crew/src/audio-fx.ts now wires a shared `masterDuckNode` (a GainNode created lazily during ensureContext, starting at gain=1.0) between every gameplay event and ctx.destination. On match-won / match-lost / match-draw, `duckFor(c, 0.6, 0.3)` snaps the duck gain to 0.3 then linearRampToValueAtTime(1, now+0.6) — anything playing through the duck node during that window inherits the attenuation, then naturally rises back. Match chimes themselves bypass the duck (connectOutput's `bypassDuck=true` path) by routing straight to ctx.destination, so the chime sits clearly on top.
verification: acceptance: When a match ends, the win/lose chime is clearly audible and any bomb / footstep events that happen during the chime feel quieter than usual; volume restores afterwards without a pop.
examples/kaboom-crew/src/audio-fx.ts: duckFor + shared masterDuckNode; connectOutput accepts bypassDuck for match chimes
+2 unit tests on the fake AudioContext: match-won schedules setValueAtTime(0.3) + linearRampToValueAtTime(1) on the duck node; bomb-place does NOT schedule a depth=0.3 call (duck stays at 1)

stories.title: Snapshot ring buffer — GET /__agf/snapshot?at=-N replays history
stories.summary: engine/runtime/start.ts now keeps a 32-entry ring buffer of WorldSnapshot captured at the end of each fixedUpdate burst. Pure helper `lookupSnapshotInRing(ring, at)` exposes the index math (at=0 → undefined / caller falls back to live, at=-1 → most-recent entry, at=-N → N steps back, |-at|>size → undefined). runtime.snapshotAt(at) wraps the helper + live-snapshot fallback; runtime.snapshotHistoryStats() returns `{ capacity, size }`. Plumbed through src/app.ts, window.__agf, and a new explicit handler in agf-dev-bridge.ts on `/snapshot` that parses `?at=` (rejecting positives with AGF_BRIDGE_INVALID_SNAPSHOT_AT) and translates the page-bridge's `{ outOfRange: true, capacity, size }` envelope into HTTP 400 AGF_PROBE_SNAPSHOT_OUT_OF_RANGE.
verification: acceptance: GET /__agf/snapshot returns the live snapshot; GET /__agf/snapshot?at=-1 returns the previous fixedUpdate's snapshot; GET /__agf/snapshot?at=1 returns 400 AGF_BRIDGE_INVALID_SNAPSHOT_AT; GET /__agf/snapshot?at=-99 returns 400 AGF_PROBE_SNAPSHOT_OUT_OF_RANGE.
engine/runtime/start.ts: SNAPSHOT_HISTORY_CAPACITY=32; ring push after each fixedUpdate burst; snapshotAt + snapshotHistoryStats on the runtime handle
engine/dev/agf-dev-bridge.ts: explicit `/snapshot` handler parses `?at=`; engine/dev/page-bridge.ts: `snapshot-at` returns `{ snapshot } | { outOfRange, capacity, size }`
+7 unit tests in tests/unit/snapshot-history-ring.test.ts: at=0 undefined; at=-1..-N indexing; out-of-range; empty ring; non-finite / positive at; non-integer floor behaviour

stories.title: Refresh docs/agent-probes.md — freecam, audio master, snapshot history
stories.summary: Pure docs sweep. Add rows for `/__agf/render/freecam`, `/__agf/audio/master-volume`, and the `at` query param on `/__agf/snapshot`. Include curl recipes + the error code for each. Verify every probe documented in the file still matches its handler signature (catch drift).
verification: acceptance: docs/agent-probes.md table-of-contents lists all three new routes; the section for each contains a working curl recipe.
Each new section calls out its error code (AGF_PROBE_SNAPSHOT_OUT_OF_RANGE, AGF_BRIDGE_INVALID_AUDIO_VOLUME, AGF_BRIDGE_INVALID_FREECAM)
